intro: Festuca centroapenninica, also known as Italian fescue, is a species of grass native to the Italian peninsula, belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Festuca centroapenninica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Festuca centroapenninica is often prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, commonly used to treat coughs, fever, and gastrointestinal disorders.
